name: n8n REST API
what_it_does: n8n's REST API for managing workflows, executions, credentials, and instances of the open-source, self-hostable workflow automation platform with code capabilities and 350+ integrations.
use_cases: Triggering n8n workflows via REST webhook or API
Managing workflow deployment and activation programmatically
Monitoring workflow execution history and error rates
Building custom automation tools on top of n8n's workflow engine
Integrating n8n into agent pipelines for orchestrated automation
not_for: Teams needing fully managed no-code tools without self-hosting
Simple single-integration tasks without complex logic
Organizations without technical staff to manage self-hosted infrastructure
best_when: You need powerful, code-capable workflow automation that can be self-hosted for data privacy, with deep customization and an MCP server for agent integration.
avoid_when: You need a simple no-code tool managed by a vendor without operational overhead.

auth.notes: API key generated in n8n settings. Passed as X-N8N-API-KEY header. Full admin access with API key. n8n Cloud uses same approach; self-hosted can also use HTTP Basic Auth.

pricing.paid_tiers: n8n Cloud: Starter $24/month (2.5k workflow executions); Pro $60/month (10k executions); Enterprise: custom
pricing.notes: Source-available (Sustainable Use License) for self-hosting. Cloud hosted plans for managed service. Enterprise license for commercial redistribution.

performance.rate_limits: Self-hosted: no limits; Cloud: execution count limits per plan

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API errors are well-structured JSON with error messages and status codes. Workflow execution errors include node-level detail helpful for debugging.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Triggering the same webhook creates separate executions. No built-in idempotency.
known_agent_gotchas: Self-hosted n8n requires a public URL for webhook triggers from external services
Fair-code license restricts commercial redistribution — check license terms if building a product on n8n
Node execution order and error handling require careful workflow design
MCP server (n8n-mcp) enables AI agents to run n8n workflows but is a community project
Webhook workflow vs API-trigger workflow have different semantics — choose the right trigger type
n8n handles credentials securely — never pass credentials as workflow parameters, use credential store

security_score_components.security_notes: API key auth for REST API. Self-hosted n8n has full control over security. n8n Cloud has SOC2. MCP server integration allows agents to trigger n8n workflows bidirectionally. Credentials stored in n8n vault.
